danger_signals.visual_form: Nazi soldiers rendered as 2D sprites with distinct uniforms and weapons visible from multiple angles
danger_signals.meaning: Armed hostiles require immediate engagement or evasion

danger_signals.visual_form: Animated canine sprites with aggressive stance

danger_signals.meaning: Progression gated by key collection; area contains protected threats or treasure

danger_signals.visual_form: Low ammunition count in HUD; empty weapon slots
danger_signals.meaning: Player vulnerability increases; must be strategic

danger_signals.visual_form: Health percentage in HUD decreasing; visual screen damage effects
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate threat to survival; death results in level restart

danger_signals.visual_form: Final level features distinctly larger or mechanically unique enemy sprite
danger_signals.meaning: Episode culmination; heightened danger requiring sustained engagement

safety_signals.visual_form: Item sprites scattered in levels and secret rooms
safety_signals.meaning: Player can recover from damage; safety available through exploration

safety_signals.visual_form: Weapon and ammo sprites placed in rooms and on dead enemies
Wall tiles that visually recede when activated; hidden room entrances
safety_signals.meaning: Safe spaces containing treasure and supplies; rewards exploration
Death penalty mitigation; safety margin against failure

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated elevator tile at level end
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression point; level completion imminent

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nazi insignia visible on soldier sprites; swastika symbols on architectural elements and decorations
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Standard guards, SS soldiers, officers, dogs; higher-tier variants with better weapons and armor appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Castle bunker architecture with Nazi banners; prison cell design; military installation aesthetics; Germanic fortress settings

faction_indicators.color_identity: Military khaki/tan (implied through protagonist William Blazkowicz as Allied spy)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Escaped prisoner status; castle prison environments; military intelligence mission contexts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sparse enemy placement; plentiful ammunition and health pickups; open room layouts with minimal obstacles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol slowly; limited aggressive response ranges

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Increased enemy density; clustered guard placements in rooms; denser wall mazes; reduced pickup availability
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies detect player at greater distances; coordinated attacks from multiple angles

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single dominant enemy sprite of larger visual scale; arena-like room design; minimal environmental cover
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss executes unique attack patterns; higher damage output; aggressive pursuit

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: All environmental design reflects military bunker/castle fortress with swastika banners, Nazi iconography, armed guard presence omnipresent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All humanoid NPCs are hostile enemies; no neutral or friendly characters; pure combat paradigm

environmental_storytelling.example: Each level explicitly themed after Nazi bunkers and military buildings; prison cells establish escape narrative context
Guard density and patrol patterns imply military security protocols; boss encounters positioned as command center or research facility climaxes
Swastika symbols and Nazi imagery on walls, doors, and decorations establish faction control and wartime setting without exposition
Episode titles ('Escape from Castle Wolfenstein', 'Operation: Eisenfaust', 'Die, Führer, Die!') communicate mission objectives and narrative stakes through text
Gold, jewels, and valuables scattered throughout Nazi facilities suggest looting occupied territory; points reward this collection

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Patrol sprite animation loops; limited movement range
Sprite animation changes to combat stance; fast movement toward player; weapon discharge visible
High-speed sprite movement toward player; aggressive lunge animation
Distinctive sprite animation with unique attack patterns; projectile or melee assault visible; movement constrained to arena bounds
HUD percentage critical; visual screen effects may intensify (empirical implementation varies by port)

notes: Spear of Destiny is a standalone retail expansion episode for Wolfenstein 3D, released by FormGen. The game communicates world-state and danger through: (1) visual enemy density and placement variation across difficulty progression, (2) environmental architecture that visually encodes Nazi military occupation, (3) sprite-based 2D character art that signals faction identity through uniform and insignia, (4) HUD resource meters (health, ammo) that directly map internal game state to player perception, (5) secret areas and hidden rooms that reward spatial exploration with narrative suggestion of espionage operations. The paradigm is pure combat with no diplomacy or faction negotiation—all non-player entities are visually and behaviorally hostile. Political situation is communicated implicitly: WWII Allied spy escaping Nazi-controlled fortress. No dialogue or exposition; visual/behavioral language dominant.
